Patch Set 7: Code-Review-2

Many thanks to Honghui for his dedication and hard work.

The following are my comments. Please understand that the purpose is for our common goal of a stable platform (S3P), and to make a better quality of Beijing (R2) Release.

While this feature and spec talks about specific technique (Pecan) for performance improvement, which sounds very good, I really worried about how it impacts overall S3P goal, and how it improves the quality R2 deliverable if we view it in a bigger picture.

S3P means Stability, Scalability, Security and Performance. It is the goal of Beijing Release (R2). However, this spec seems to address a small portion of Performance aspects. Thus the specific questions are:
- which performance metrics and benchmarks are targeted?
- which use cases are targeted that meets above performance metrics and benchmarks?
- which conditions are prerequisites to achieve the targeted performance metrics?
- How do they compare to benchmarks?
- When we consider performance improvement, have we considered its other impacts, especially adversary impacts on other platform metrics such as:
* How does it impact or not impact platform stability?
* How does it impact or not impact platform scalability?
* How does it impact or not impact platform security?

Given those unknowns, I really worried.

I suggest that we de-scope it from Beijing Release (R2) in order not to have an adversary impact on a quality Beijing Release. Instead, it should start with a PoC, understand all those metrics, benchmarks and potential impacts, test with real use cases, collect all relevant test data regarding S3P metrics, establish a data model, normalize the data collection and analyze the data so that we can completely understand its benefits and potential positive/negative impacts on the platform.

Thus I gave -2.

 > benchmarks and potential impacts, test with real use cases, collect
 > all relevant test data regarding S3P metrics, establish a data
 > model, normalize the data collection and analyze the data so that
 > we can completely understand its benefits and potential
 > positive/negative impacts on the platform.
 > 
 > Thus I gave -2.

Hi Bin.


First, it is not Pecan which is the specific technology. It is mainly eventlet+wsgi that bump up the performance. 


And, as we previously discussed, S3P improvement is too big for one spec. So, I think it is good to just focus on Performance aspect, as long as there is no negative impact on other aspects, right?


For all these questions, I should already have described them in the spec. For example, performance metric is about improve the speed of handling api request.  If you think there is not properly described, I guess the better way is add comment inline, and I will re-word it. If you find there is any missing description, I will add them to spec.


And lastly,  this spec will NOT impact what we already have in multi-cloud(also mentioned in the spec), because of the way how we plan to implement it. I see no reason to delay or de-scope it in B release.

Patch Set 7:

Honghui,

I am really sorry for your feeling of work being delayed. Although the feeling may not be correct, the most important is to do things right.

Because of fundamental issue in the methodology, it is very challenging to comment inline. You may see why as I comment more as follows.

(1) What is the best technical option to improve performance

Thank you for clarifying that it is eventlet+wsgi.

In the spec, there are 2 options, one is current django and the other one is eventlet+pecan (line 60). on Jan 28, there was a comment that "nginx + uwsgi to improve the performance by supporting concurrent API processing. We don't need another framework to address this issue.". And your response on Jan 30 was that "it might not be ideal api framework. I will add more details in new version, we can carry on discussion in new version."

Maybe I misunderstood. But I was expecting a comparison between nginx+uwsgi and eventlet+pecan, so that we can know which one is the best choice technically, and what are the impacts on current framework and amount of work for framework evolution.

However, entire Option 1 (Line 29-54) is talking about current django framework, and its cons. Option 2 is talking about eventlet+pecan. I didn't see any other options/solutions be discussed, analyzed, compared before we can definitively say let's go with eventlet+pecan.

If there is indeed no other options, it is good. But there was proposal about the option of nginx+uwsgi, and django+celery, I am just curious why those 2 options are ignored? If eventlet+pecan is indeed the best option, can we list all of those options, give a deep dive of technical pros and cons, and analyze what is the most applicable to our framework?

I am indeed not convinced why your solution is the best and most applicable to our framework.

(2) The implementation of your proposal is not mature for R2

On Jan 24, yourself already recognized that "And I don't think this api framework will be mature in R2." when you responded to another comment.

I understand that it is configurable option, and secondary to current framework. However, there is still risk of putting an immature and unstable solution into Beijing release, which is targeted for S3P - Stability is the most important.

Thus I am wondering what is the rationale here? Why cannot we start with a PoC, and put it into release after it is stable and mature? If we know it is unstable and immature, why cannot we wait until it is stable and mature?
